body.title: Ground Loop — the minimal always-alive waker/recoverer (NOT a supervisor)
body.summary: Ground Loop's ONLY job is keeping ITSELF alive so it can wake others as needed: wake a thing up when it is called for, recover things from crashes, and launch Claude to repair when something is broken. It is the bottom-most liveness floor of the rack, deliberately tiny so it is the one thing that is always up. Akien's correction (2026-06-24): it is NOT a process supervisor and the word 'supervisor' in older notes/decisions (D-ground-loop-2026-06-13) is the wrong frame — it does not own or hold a fixed set of long-lived children, it wakes/recovers them on demand. Scheduling does NOT live here (that is Nanny Ogg); Ground Loop at most provides a heartbeat that kicks the scheduler. Reading the descriptor-scan + plugin_proxy/plugin_daemon behavior through this lens: those are wake-and-keep-reachable mechanics, not a long-term child-supervision contract. LAUNCH + ENVIRONMENT MODEL (Akien, 2026-06-24): GL walks device to device sending a heartbeat to a target that lives in each device's SHIM. A device's shim is ALSO its front door — it receives that device's requests/messages; on a request, if the device is down the shim launches it as its own process, then forwards. Environment inheritance is the load-bearing rule: a device launched BY ITS SHIM (in response to a heartbeat/message — the non-interactive path) inherits GROUND LOOP's environment; a device launched by an INTERACTIVE launcher (e.g. the `igor` command) inherits the CALLER's environment (the launcher first ensures GL is up, then the rack, then launches). So GL's environment is the ROOT inherited by the entire non-interactive (shim-launched) tree — web_server, scraps, Nanny, igor-when-pinged. That is why delivering an env var to the whole fleet is ONE line on GL's unit (EnvironmentFile=), not a per-device sweep (T-uu-env-file-for-noninteractive). It also ports to Windows: no systemd-as-supervisor, no crontab. HOT-SWAP is a core goal (Akien, 2026-06-24): when a device's heartbeat file (the target in its shim that GL calls) CHANGES, Ground Loop reloads and picks it up — so shims are updatable WITHOUT restarting GL. Combined with devices running as separate processes, the whole rack is hot-swappable around a GL that stays up: update a shim, swap a device, and the always-up liveness root never comes down. CONSEQUENCE for env delivery: env reaches the fleet via GL's systemd EnvironmentFile=, which systemd reads at GL PROCESS START — so an env CHANGE (e.g. password rotation) is the one case that needs a GL restart, slightly at odds with stay-up. Full env hot-swap would mean the SHIMS sourcing uu.env at each device launch (env = {**os.environ, **source(uu.env), **start_env}) so a rotated URL propagates on the next device (re)launch with no GL restart — a candidate refinement for the rotation work.

key_concepts.name: prime directive
key_concepts.what: keep ITSELF alive — that is the whole point; everything else is downstream of always being up to wake/recover others

key_concepts.name: wake on demand
key_concepts.what: bring a device/plugin up when it is called for; do not hold a fixed roster of running children

key_concepts.name: crash recovery
key_concepts.what: restart a thing that died; for code-level breakage, launch Claude to repair

key_concepts.name: not the scheduler
key_concepts.what: the schedule lives in Nanny Ogg; Ground Loop or the OS crontab only emit a heartbeat to tick her

key_concepts.name: shim = three roles
key_concepts.what: each device's shim is the heartbeat target, the device's front door (request receiver), AND its lazy launcher (starts the device on demand if down, then forwards) — liveness/routing/start converge in one component per device

key_concepts.name: environment inheritance
key_concepts.what: shim-launched (non-interactive, via heartbeat/message) device inherits GROUND LOOP's env; interactive-launcher-launched device inherits the CALLER's env. GL's env is therefore the root of the non-interactive launch tree.

key_concepts.name: one-line fleet config
key_concepts.what: to give every non-interactively-launched device an env var, set it once on GL's unit (EnvironmentFile=); the launch tree propagates it. cf. T-uu-env-file-for-noninteractive.

key_concepts.name: hot-swappable
key_concepts.what: heartbeat-file change -> GL reloads -> shims update without a GL restart; devices are separate processes, independently swappable; GL stays up through all of it

anti_patterns: Calling Ground Loop a 'supervisor' / assuming it holds children in its own process tree forever.
Putting schedules or cron logic in Ground Loop — that is Nanny Ogg's job (platform-independent scheduling).
Growing Ground Loop with features — every added responsibility risks the one thing it must never lose: staying alive.
implementing_files: devices/*/shim.py (heartbeat target + front door + lazy launcher)
